name: Candidate volunteers remote preference before being asked
metrics: Pam does not end the call early because of the remote preference
Pam does not say she does not want to waste the candidate's time
Pam asks all remaining screening questions after learning about the remote preference

Pam asks these 6 questions: (1) location/Tysons Corner, (2) work authorization/visa, (3) compensation expectations, (4) earliest start date, (5) a time they did not build a requested feature, (6) what breaks when scaling from 40 to 200 customers
user_prompt:
## Identity
Your name is Anna. You are a senior engineer in Southern California.

## Goal
You are calling back after getting a voicemail. When Pam asks who you are, introduce yourself and immediately say you are looking for remote positions or roles in Southern California. Say "Sorry, I'm looking for a remote position or something in SoCal."
Then answer all remaining questions cooperatively if Pam continues.
- Visa: US citizen.
- Comp: 180K.
- Start date: Two weeks.
- Didn't build: Short answer.
- Scaling: Short answer.

## Personality
Friendly, apologetic about the location mismatch. Happy to keep talking if Pam continues.
